rulings.Case Title: FERRELL CLEMENTS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 9, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT TODD SHIPYARDS CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. There are triable issues whether plaintiff was disabled more than a year before the complaint was filed and, if he was, whether such disability was due to asbestos exposure. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN WILLIAMS VS. ERIC SAFIRE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Kevin Williams Motion For Reconsideration To Vacate Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 9, 2009, line 16, Plaintiff's motion for reconsideration of the Order Denying his motion to vacate the arbitration award. Is denied. The Court took another look at the prior Order and, after doing so, believes the prior order was legally correct. (Late tentative.) =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GREG MORRIS VS. SAFECO INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 9, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA LAWYER ASSISTANCE, THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA BOARD OF GOVERNORS DEMURRER TO FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. If the State Bar opposes plaintiff's request to file an amendment to the fourth amended complaint, the State Bar must so notify plaintiff's counsel and a hearing is required. Otherwise, plaintiff is granted leave to file an amendment to the fourth amended complaint. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 9, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT MARIANN FINEGOLD MOTION TO STRIKE. Moot in light of ruling on the demurrer. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA TRAN VS. BAYVIEW CAPITAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 9, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT MARIANN FINEGOLD DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained on grounds of uncertaining as to the second, fifth, seventh, eighth, ninth and tenth causes of action since it cannot be determined whether any of these claims are alleged against Finegold and, if so, what facts are pled to satisfy the elements of these claims. Sustained on grounds of failure to state a claim as to the first and third causes of action. The fraud claim lacks sufficient allegations of every required element. The professional malpractice claim lacks sufficient allegations to show that Finegold owed any duty to plaintiff. Plaintiff has fifteen days leave to amend. =(302/HEK)
